mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS is in the lower regime.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.2% indicates deep accommodation
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 9.5% signals labor market slack and economic weakness that depresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when UNRATE crosses above 9.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 9.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 366404M and HOUST at 626K units
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint RSAFS and HOUST growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 102.0 signal supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which constrains profit margins and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 102.27 is near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO < 102.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth interacts with unemployment rate (UNRATE) such that simultaneous payroll expansion and UNRATE decline signal robust labor market tightening. This interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as wage pressures and hiring confidence boost equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month AND UNRATE declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 130161K and UNRATE at 9.9%
regime_hint: labor market recovery with concurrent employment gains and unemployment decline
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 220 combined with FEDFUNDS below 1.0% signals real negative rates that erode fixed-income returns and redirect capital to equities. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in financial repression environments.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 217.73 and FEDFUNDS at 0.2%
regime_hint: financial repression with negative real rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in financial repression regime are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly predicts S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through a direct earnings channel: higher GDP growth expands corporate revenues and profits. This always-on mechanism operates across all regimes with positive direction.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 13254.7B in chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals broad-based economic expansion that raises profit expectations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when both series expand simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month AND PAYEMS increases month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 102.27 and PAYEMS at 130161K
regime_hint: broad-based economic expansion with synchronized supply and labor growth
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint INDPRO and PAYEMS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
